testName: Log in and open skill, grab lock, and edit skill on browser 1 with user 1
errorPattern: stale element reference: Can't call click on element with selector "[data-testid="Take skill lock"]"

fix.summary: After clicking Toggle edit mode, detect the steal-lock modal via execute(), then click 'Take skill lock' with document.querySelector + execute (not stored element refs). Sidebar refactor causes React re-renders that invalidate WebDriver element IDs.
fix.pattern: In getLock(): await mBrowser.pause(3000); const hasModal = await mBrowser.execute(() => !!document.querySelector('[data-testid="Take skill lock"]')); if (hasModal) { await mBrowser.execute(() => document.querySelector('[data-testid="Take skill lock"]')?.click()); } Optionally assert via Sauce GQL helper (stealSkillLock). Aligns with git fix 9660335.

investigationFindings: Git history (commit 9660335 area) explicitly fixes skill-lock flakiness by replacing element-reference clicks with browser.execute after a sidebar refactor. Multi-remote stresses timing (both sessions race), making the stale-element window wider than single-browser tests.
rootCauseChain: TIMING_ISSUE -> React re-render (sidebar refactor) -> stored element reference goes stale -> click on lock modal fails
